subject: RE: Project Meridian — Data Mapping Document v1 — Review Comments + Revised Draft

messages.body:
Tom, Kevin,

Attached are our review comments on the data mapping v1, along with a revised draft (v1.1) that incorporates our proposed corrections.

Summary of changes:

**Section 3 (ADT Mappings) — Changes Required:**
1. PID-18 → identifier.value: Added system discriminator (urn:oid:2.16.840.1.113883.4.6) to prevent MRN collision. Please update your transformation logic.
2. PID-19 (SSN) handling: Added explicit null/dummy check — if value is 999-99-9999, omit from output. See row 47.
3. A03 (Discharge): Added missing discharge disposition code mapping → Encounter.hospitalization.dischargeDisposition. This is required for billing. See rows 88-94.

**Section 4 (Custom Lab Extensions) — Changes Required:**
4. Extension #7 (result_comment_routing_flag): Change cardinality from 0..1 to 0..*
5. Extension #12 (external_lab_source_id): Change type from string to Coding. We've included the reference lab code system in the attachment.

**NEEDS_CLARIFICATION Fields — Resolved:**
- PV1-50 (Alternate Visit ID): Map to Encounter.identifier with use = 'secondary'. ✓
- PV1-51 (Visit Indicator): Map to Encounter.class — valid values are 'I' (inpatient), 'O' (outpatient), 'E' (emergency). ✓
- PV1-52 (Other Healthcare Provider): Map to Encounter.participant with participantType='PART' when populated; omit when null (~60% of records). ✓

We'd like to get your revised v1.2 back by January 27th so we can do a final sign-off before the end of the month.

Please confirm receipt — we're watching the timeline closely.

Marcus Webb
Operations Lead, Acme Health

[Attachment: Meridian_DataMapping_v1.1_Review_Comments_20260120.xlsx — 1.1MB]
[Attachment: External_Lab_Reference_Lab_Codes_v2.csv — 42KB]

attachments.description: Revised data mapping with embedded comments and tracked changes
Reference lab code system for Extension #12 Coding type

note: DataBridge (Tom Reyes and Kevin Park) have not replied to this email as of 2026-01-28. No read receipt, no acknowledgment, no out-of-office. This email contains critical review comments that DataBridge must address before the data mapping can be approved.
